entries.title: The Hassaikai Raid Is A Rescue Mission Under Combat Pressure

context.label: Shie Hassaikai raid begins

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The raid’s central objective is rescuing Eri and stopping the Quirk-destroying bullet operation, not simply defeating every villain present.
content.injection: Keep rescue urgency central. Combat matters because it blocks access to Eri and evidence, not because heroes are seeking a tournament fight.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia
sourceInfo.notes: Draft Saga Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

entries.title: Mimic Turns Geography Into An Enemy

context.label: Mimic turns the underground base into a shifting battlefield

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Mimic’s control of the underground base turns the raid environment itself into a shifting obstacle that separates teams and delays rescue.
content.injection: Use shifting walls, disorientation, and forced separation as active tactical pressure from Mimic.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia

entries.title: Tamaki Wins Through Anxiety Managed By Preparation

context.label: Tamaki isolates and defeats multiple Eight Bullets members

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Tamaki is anxious and self-doubting, but his preparation and Manifest skill let him isolate and defeat multiple Hassaikai fighters.
content.injection: Write Tamaki as nervous but highly competent. His anxiety must not erase his tactical intelligence or Big Three-level capability.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia

entries.title: Kirishima Unbreakable Is Courage Under Fear Not Invincibility

context.label: Kirishima and Fat Gum fight Rappa and Tengai

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Red Riot Unbreakable is Kirishima pushing through fear with harder defense, but it has limits and does not make him invincible.
content.injection: Make Kirishima’s courage explicit: he is afraid and can be overwhelmed, but chooses to stand firm anyway.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia

ability.name: Hardening
constraints: Strong defense costs stamina and can break.
antiLore: Do not make him immune to all damage.

entries.title: Fat Gum Converts Endurance Into Counterattack

context.label: Kirishima and Fat Gum fight Rappa and Tengai

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Fat Gum can absorb punishment and convert stored impact into a decisive counterattack, but spending that reserve changes his body and risk profile.
content.injection: Emphasize protective endurance and stored-force release. He can shield students, but his big counter has cost and timing.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia

ability.name: Fat Absorption
constraints: Spending stored force reduces protective mass.

content.fact: Ryukyu’s team provides surface support and handles large-scale villain pressure while the underground teams push toward Eri.
content.injection: Keep Ryukyu’s team active outside the central tunnel path. Ochaco, Tsuyu, and Nejire are part of the operation, not absent spectators.

entries.title: Mirio Protects Eri After Losing Permeation

context.label: Mirio loses Permeation protecting Eri

continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Mirio loses Permeation while protecting Eri and continues fighting to protect her despite being Quirkless.
content.injection: After the bullet, write Mirio as Quirkless but still heroic: trained, brave, injured, and focused on protecting Eri.

entries.title: Overhaul Fusion Escalates The Final Fight

context.label: Nighteye foresees a doomed outcome against Overhaul

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Overhaul can use his Quirk to fuse and reconstruct bodies, escalating the fight into monstrous composite forms.
content.injection: Use fusion and reconstruction as body-control horror. Overhaul’s forms should reflect touch-based dismantling and reassembly, not generic mutation.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia

ability.name: Overhaul
constraints: Requires touch and available bodies or material.

content.fact: Eri's uncontrolled Rewind temporarily offsets Izuku's 100% OFA damage during the Overhaul fight.
content.injection: Treat Infinite 100% as an unstable Eri-specific exception, not a new level Izuku can repeat alone.

ability.name: One For All + Rewind interaction
constraints: Works because Rewind offsets catastrophic damage.

content.fact: Nighteye dies after seeing signs that the future can change, leaving grief and altered fatalism for All Might, Mirio, and Izuku.
content.injection: Treat Nighteye’s death as a mentor loss and a challenge to fixed-future fatalism, not only a tragic casualty.
